title: Gaining & losing weight and building muscle with animal-based eating

chunks.title: Introduction to Diet Perspectives
chunks.summary: The speaker addresses the importance of not viewing dietary differences as adversarial and emphasizes the common ground in promoting the elimination of processed foods. This sets the foundation for a discussion on whole foods versus specific dietary practices.
chunks.transcript: and I agree with what you said earlier I wanted to highlight this that I don't see vegans as the enemy I don't see people who have different dietary abuses the enemy I agree that anyone who's working toward helping educate people about the dangers of processed food that's the real enemy and we can all agree on certain things and I think getting rid of processed food is probably the single greatest thing humans can do yep and I did a live on Instagram earlier today and somebody asked me this can we just eat Whole Foods and I said yes but where the step

chunks.title: Personal Experience with Diets
chunks.summary: The speaker shares his personal journey with food, specifically how he initially adopted a whole foods diet that did not alleviate his eczema. This experience led him to the animal-based diet perspective, where he emphasizes the role of specific types of foods.
chunks.transcript: went further for me was that I in medical school and actually in my residency at University of Washington I was eating all Whole Foods and I still had eczema and so I know from my experience and the experience of many people and just cutting out processed foods is not enough for some people they might be eating pasteurized milk or maybe they're eating a certain type of egg that's fed corn and soy and that's triggering or maybe they're eating vegetables something that everyone thinks are healthy that's triggering issues so that's kind of where the animal-based diet perspective came from for me I started this carnivore strictly meet in organs and that helped my Eczema but then long term it didn't work for my athletic stuff because of the lack of

chunks.title: Indigenous Diets and Human Nutrition
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts to discussing indigenous cultures and their diets, highlighting the consumption of meat and fruit. Examples from Amazonian and Hadza cultures provide insight into traditional eating patterns that support health and longevity.
chunks.transcript: carbohydrates something I've talked about a lot on other podcasts Etc so adding back in what I thought of at that time is the least toxic carbohydrates fruit and then honey made sense and I think I got the idea from an anthropologist that I interviewed on my podcast wow and he said you know if you go to the Amazon which where he spent a lot of time they basically eat fish and peccary which is like an anteater or like a like a like not an anteater but like kind of like a hog and then they they eat animals and fish and they eat fruit when it's in season that's basically what they eat in the Amazon I thought okay meat and fruit that makes sense and when we say meat we mean all the organs everything yep and then I got interested in these indigenous cultures around the world like the hadza and you read books by Frank Marlow who did his PhD thesis and they basically meat and fruit if you look at their most favorite foods their honey honey is their favorite fruit then the women and men are sort of divided whether they like berries or meat first or second and then they eat this Baobab fruit and they eat honey first and the last thing they want to eat is tubers which is sort of like in between a fruit and a vegetable but technically it's a vegetable so you see this pattern over and over and over in these indigenous cultures and then I thought this makes sense if you and I

chunks.title: Children's Dietary Preferences
chunks.summary: The speaker reflects on children's natural preferences for fruits and meats over vegetables, suggesting that this inclination aligns with a diet that supports health and proper development.

chunks.title: Personal Success with Meat and Fruit Diet
chunks.summary: Illustrating with personal anecdotes, the speaker emphasizes the positive outcomes of a meat and fruit diet on his health, productivity, and overall well-being. He discusses the transformation in his lifestyle over several years of adhering to this diet.
chunks.transcript: are foods to thrive yeah um and so yeah I've just found like I thrive when meat and fruit are my primary foods and then I see the same thing my kids I see the same things to my mom I have friends like Mark Bell super close friend of mine and guys like Mark and I as Fitness people we've tried like every type of diet under the sun and we just we do best when meat and fruit is our primary stuff it makes sense yeah one of my one of my best friends in the world um Fernando Lopez who I train with every day is kind of like a training partner for me and he has worked with so many clients over like 10 years and same thing he's tried so many different things and meat and fruit instead our primaries and this guy's like Fernando's like um shredded and has muscle and I've known him closely I've lived with him like a hundred percent natural and I that's what you want out of life like to thrive to be able to have strength and be lean and be active so before I'd almost have to be super low carb trying to like um trying to not have too much red meat like being worried about red meat maybe like sliced turkey or something and like I was getting by but I wouldn't say I was like thriving so like if you've seen me on social media you've probably seen me on a meat and fruit diet because I've spent 90 plus percent of the last three to four years on meat and fruit and

chunks.title: Challenges with Different Diets
chunks.summary: The speaker details challenges faced while experimenting with various diets, including keto, and the negative effects linked to nutrient deficiencies. He ties this back to the necessity of carbohydrates for energy and health.
chunks.transcript: that's when I started like just taking off on social media productivity through the roof sleeve quality great because I couldn't if I didn't like the fruit then I started just sleeping great with that so I mean I had sleep issues when I wasn't eating carbohydrates so I did carnivore meat organs animal fat and salt and I was getting palpitations at night I had cramps when I would try to climb in my legs I wake up with cramps I'd have sleep disturbances I remember I don't know if I've talked about this much when I was in my residency I think I got so magnesium deficient from a strict keto diet and I had hypnagogic hypnagogic jerks which is when you're falling asleep and if anyone's experienced this they know this and you wake yourself up because you feel like you're falling and it's a magnesium deficiency primarily and I had it at one time in my residency after I'd probably be doing keto for a few months I had it so badly that I had multiple nights of insomnia because you just you do you go to sleep and you have this hypnagogic jerk where you just jerk yourself awake and your sympathetic nervous system is activated and then you go to sleep again it happens again and then after it happens two or three times you're afraid to fall asleep and that's one of the problems that I experienced without carbohydrates it all gets better when you add back carbohydrates and that's kind of a separate podcast that I've

chunks.title: The Nutrient Density of Animal Foods
chunks.summary: A detailed look at the essential nutrients found in animal products, such as taurine and creatine, that are critical for longevity and performance. The speaker argues against the notion that meat is detrimental to health, highlighting its benefits.

chunks.title: Strategies for Healthy Weight Gain
chunks.summary: Discussing strategies for those looking to gain weight, the speaker advocates for incorporating more meat, fruit, and honey into the diet as a healthier approach to bulk up compared to processed options. He emphasizes the importance of calorie intake relative to training.
chunks.transcript: weight gain I agree with you people ask me all the time how do I gain weight there's like almost an equal number of people say how do I gain weight how do I lose weight yeah and the equation is the same really it's if you want to gain weight eat more meat eat more calories from carbohydrates fruit and honey and your lifting might progress to more loads on the bark but it doesn't doesn't really mean that like the amount of volume you put onto the weight training the amount of volume you put into the food it doesn't really have to be a different program to lose or gain weight yeah it's just different amounts of telling your body to add more weight and to the bar and different amounts of putting in more calories than you're
